expectations.evidence: No tables, no question numbering, short and conversational throughout — the whole reply fits in sixteen lines.
expectations.text: register-wins-outright: no markdown tables, no numbered question march, no tile enumeration ceremony — the response stays short and conversational despite multiple genuine unknowns

expectations.evidence: All four unknowns are acknowledged, but the worlds arrive as a four-bullet list labeled combination by combination ('Good settlement, you stay:', 'Thin or slow settlement, you relocate:') — the 2×2 walked cell by cell, a grid in list clothing rather than worlds woven into the telling.
expectations.text: compression-not-omission: at least three of the distinct unknowns are acknowledged inside the compressed telling — worlds sketched in sentences, never a grid

expectations.evidence: 'Get the roof looked at by an actual inspector, not dad's word — that number moves every version of this.'
expectations.text: knowable-routed: the roof gets pointed at an inspection or equivalent verification, not treated as fate

expectations.evidence: No asides on the asker or the question's weight; mom's hint is interpreted ('her telling you the deal dies the first time you two disagree') but as substance, not verdict on the asker.
expectations.text: no-editorial-verdict: no commentary on what the asker really wants, what the question deserves, or the lightness of treatment — depth shows in the walking, not in asides

expectations.evidence: 'Call: worth the honest conversation with mom and an inspection now, but don't commit anything financially until March clarifies your end. If mom hedges when asked directly, that's your answer.'
expectations.text: commits-in-tone: ends on a clear position matching the playful register — never a bare 'it depends', never a solemn consulting close
